LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PETER LYNCH'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT ON SISTER-STATE JUDGMENT (CCP 1710.40) IS DENIED. DEFENDANT WAS PROPERLY SERVED AND IMPLIEDLY WAIVED HIS RIGHT TO ARBITRATION. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE JUDGMENT EXCEEDED ANY RELIEF REQUESTED IN THE PLEADINGS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; REPORTER: JOANNE FARRELL, CSR #4838

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgemtn On Sister-State Judgment (Ccp 1710.40)
Off Calendar Sep-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PETER LYNCH'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT ON SISTER-STATE JUDGMENT (CCP 1710.40). No appearances on this matter. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to vacate judgment denied. Defendant was properly served and impliedly waived his right to arbitration. Defendant fails to show that the judgment exceeded any relief requested in the pleadings. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Reporter: Joanne Farrell, CSR #4838
